title: Inflection points intro
description: Analyze the points of inflection of a function given the function's graph.
topic_path: Determining concavity of intervals and finding points of inflection: graphical

assessment_items.question_content:
Let $f$ be a twice differentiable function.

![Function f is graphed. The x-axis is unnumbered. The graph consists of a curve. The curve starts in quadrant 2, moves downward with decreasing steepness through point A on the negative x-axis to a point in quadrant 3, moves upward with increasing steepness through point B to point C at the origin, moves downward with increasing steepness through point D to a point in quadrant 4, then moves upward with increasing steepness, ending in quadrant 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0db8c4d479edcfcb650e4eb7c185da4dbf592a7f)

**Which of the labeled points are inflection points of $f$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An inflection point is a point where the graph of the function changes concavity (from up to down or vice versa).

In general, graphs that are concave up have a cup $\cup$ shape and graphs that are concave down have a cap $\cap$ shape.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The graph of $f$ changes its concavity at points $B$ and $D$.

![The graph of function f changes from concave up to concave down at point B and from concave down to concave up at point D. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c217e1a424a5b68862cd5a1a3d0aa8cec74c6bac)

choices.content: The graph has no inflection points.

assessment_items.question_content:
Let $g$ be a twice differentiable function.

![Function g is graphed. The x-axis is unnumbered. The graph consists of a curve. The curve starts in quadrant 3, moves upward with decreasing steepness to a point in quadrant 2, moves downward with decreasing steepness to point A, continues downward with decreasing steepness to point B at the origin, continues downward with increasing steepness through point C to point D, then moves upward with increasing steepness and ends in quadrant 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c0e303e06a7529c954724392132375efe1ad2ccd)

**Which of the labeled points are inflection points of $g$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An inflection point is a point where the graph of the function changes concavity (from up to down or vice versa).

In general, graphs that are concave up have a cup $\cup$ shape and graphs that are concave down have a cap $\cap$ shape.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The graph of $g$ changes its concavity at points $A$, $B$, and $C$.

![The graph of function g changes from concave down to concave up at point A, from concave up to concave down at point B, and from concave down to concave up at point C.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9860d2beda7eed90712367fee659ef7e72b7abe9)

choices.content: The graph has no inflection points.

assessment_items.question_content:
Let $h$ be a twice differentiable function.

![Function h is graphed. The x-axis is unnumbered. The graph consists of a curve. The curve starts in quadrant 2, moves upward with decreasing steepness to point A, moves downward with decreasing steepness through point B to point C at the origin, then moves upward with increasing steepness through point D, ending in quadrant 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f25586b6b0ae3305712b2a61f1d79329a0a09676)

**Which of the labeled points are inflection points of $h$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An inflection point is a point where the graph of the function changes concavity (from up to down or vice versa).

In general, graphs that are concave up have a cup $\cup$ shape and graphs that are concave down have a cap $\cap$ shape.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The graph of $h$ changes its concavity at point $B$.

![The graph of function h changes from concave down to concave up at point B. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c592c2dd49c51282e7af038c3e1292e0bed757db)

choices.content: The graph has no inflection points.

assessment_items.question_content:
Let $f$ be a twice differentiable function.

![Function f is graphed. The x-axis is unnumbered. The graph consists of a curve. The curve starts in quadrant 2, moves downward with decreasing steepness through point A on the negative x-axis and point B on the negative y-axis to point C in quadrant 4, then moves upward with increasing steepness through point D on the positive x-axis, ending in quadrant 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1af468bd895198368033d402075e9ed518acd032)

**Which of the labeled points are inflection points of $f$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
An inflection point is a point where the graph of the function changes concavity (from up to down or vice versa).

In general, graphs that are concave up have a cup $\cup$ shape and graphs that are concave down have a cap $\cap$ shape.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The graph of $f$ does not change its concavity at any points shown.

The graph has no inflection points.

![The graph of function f is concave up.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7301d872899282fa447c9dc3a317a2da15811b7b)

choices.content: The graph has no inflection points.
